figures.caption: Figure 1: Comparison of equilibrium and CE expanded reduced distribution functions in the strongly rarefied cylinder wake.

Figure 2: Hypersonic flow at Kn∞ = 0.02 and Ma∞ = 5 passing over a circular cylinder by the GKS and UGKS. Distributions along the stagnation line: (a) pressure, (b) temperature, and (c) x-direction velocity U .

Figure 3: Hypersonic flow at Kn∞ = 0.02 and Ma∞ = 5 passing over a circular cylinder by the GKS, UGKS, and UGKWP. Surface quantities distributions: (a) pressure coefficient, (b) shear stress coefficient, and (c) heat flux coefficient.
Figure 4: Hypersonic flow at Kn∞ = 0.4 and Ma∞ = 5 passing over a circular cylinder by the GKS and UGKS. Distributions of (a) pressure, (b) temperature, and (c) x-direction velocity U .

Figure 5: Hypersonic flow at Kn∞ = 0.4 and Ma∞ = 5 passing over a circular cylinder by the GKS, UGKS, and UGKWP. Surface quantities distributions: (a) pressure coefficient, (b) shear stress coefficient, and (c) heat flux coefficient.
Figure 6: Hypersonic flow at Kn∞ = 0.4 and Ma∞ = 5 past a circular cylinder (GKS). Comparison of reduced nonequilibrium and CE expansion distribution functions.
Figure 7: Mach number contours around the Apollo 6 command module at three altitudes, with streamlines on the symmetry (Z) plane.

Figure 8: Apollo 6 command module: integrated aerodynamic coefficients versus altitude H for the GKS, SUGKS, and DSMC. (a) Drag coefficient Cd, (b) lift coefficient Cl, and (c) pitching moment coefficient Cm.

Figure 9: Hypersonic flow over a 70◦ blunted cone with sting at α = 30◦, Ma∞ = 20.2, and Kn∞ = 0.013 (H ∼ 80 km) by the GKS. Contours on the symmetry plane: (a) x-direction velocity U , (b) density, and (c) temperature with streamlines.

Figure 10: Hypersonic flow over a 70◦ blunted cone with sting at α = 30◦, Ma∞ = 20.5, and Kn∞ = 5.4 × 10−4 (H ∼ 57 km) by the GKS. Contours on the symmetry plane: (a) x-direction velocity U , (b) density, and (c) temperature with streamlines.

Figure 11: Comparison of the time-averaged temperature by the UGKWP and the temperature by the GKS on the symmetry plane of the 70◦ blunted cone with sting at α = 0◦. (a) Ma∞ = 20.2, Kn∞ = 0.013 (H ∼ 80 km); (b) Ma∞ = 20.5, Kn∞ = 5.4× 10−4 (H ∼ 57 km).

Figure 12: Hypersonic flow over a 70◦ blunted cone with sting at Kn∞ = 0.013 (H ∼ 80 km) and Ma∞ = 20.2. Aerodynamic coefficients versus angle of attack: (a) axial force CA, (b) normal force CN , and (c) pitching moment coefficient Cm. Comparisons include experiment, GKS, UGKWP, DSMC, NS with slip wall [53].

Figure 13: Hypersonic flow over a 70◦ blunted cone with sting at Kn∞ = 5.4×10−4 (H ∼ 57 km) and Ma∞ = 20.5. Aerodynamic coefficients versus angle of attack: (a) axial force CA, (b) normal force CN , and (c) pitching moment coefficient Cm. Comparisons include the experiment, the UGKWP, and the GKS.

Figure 14: Hypersonic flow over 9◦ blunted cone at Kn∞ = 0.065 (H ∼ 80 km), Ma∞ = 10.15, and α = 25◦ by the GKS. Contours on the symmetry plane: (a) x-direction velocity U , (b) density, and (c) temperature with streamlines.

Figure 15: Hypersonic flow over 9◦ blunted cone at Kn∞ = 0.65 (H ∼ 94 km), Ma∞ = 10.15, and α = 25◦ by the GKS. Contours on the symmetry plane: (a) x-direction velocity U , (b) density, and (c) temperature with streamlines.
Figure 16: Comparison of the time-averaged UGKWP temperature and the GKS temperature for the 9◦ blunted cone at Ma∞ = 10.15 and α = 0◦. (a) Kn∞ = 0.065 (H ∼ 80 km); (b) Kn∞ = 0.65 (H ∼ 94 km).

Figure 17: Hypersonic flow over 9◦ blunted cone at Kn∞ = 0.065 (H ∼ 80 km), Ma∞ = 10.15. Aerodynamic coefficients versus angle of attack: (a) drag coefficient Cd, (b) lift coefficient Cl, and (c) pitching moment coefficient Cm. Comparisons include experiment [55], DSMC [56], NS with slip boundary, and the GKS.

Figure 18: Hypersonic flow over 9◦ blunted cone at Kn∞ = 0.65 (H ∼ 94 km), Ma∞ = 10.15. Aerodynamic coefficients versus angle of attack: (a) drag coefficient Cd, (b) lift coefficient Cl, and (c) pitching moment coefficient Cm. Comparisons include the UGKWP and the GKS.
